data.body: PASS 2 verification note (finding re: canary invariant `computed > 0 && applied == 0`): grounded against batch_dispatch.py:543-551 — outbound `update_one` skips a link `add` already present in either direction ("already present — no duplicate add"). On an idempotent re-sync, `computed > 0` (a link entry exists) while `applied == 0` though nothing failed. Inbound `links_applied` (apply_inbound.py:469-497) increments only on actual write, so the same idempotency class yields applied==0 with no failure. The plan's invariant as worded does not distinguish a failed no-op from a dedup-skipped no-op; worth disambiguating (e.g. count skips separately, or define `computed` as "ops attempted after dedup") before promoting to hard-fail.
